qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: What does the following program output? program module (input, output); var a:array [1...5] of integer; i, j: integer; procedure unknown (var b: integer, var c: integer); var i:integer; begin for i := 1 to 5 do a[i] := i; b:= 0; c := 0 for i := 1 to 5 do write (a[i]);

qids.prose: Indicate all the true statements from the following: A. Recursive descent parsing cannot be used for grammar with left recursion. B. qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Consider the following pseudo-code (all data items are of type integer): procedure P(a, b, c); a := 2; c := a + b; end {P} begin x := 1; y := 5; z := 100; P(x, x*y, z); Write ('x = ', x, 'z = ', z);
